memories.what: A juggling pattern built on a 'reverse cascade' base (siteswap 3), with arms crossing and uncrossing in a way that makes the balls appear to chase each other chaotically. The chaos is entirely structural — it is called a 'mess' but it obeys strict rules.
memories.why_interesting: The apparent disorder is produced by a precise underlying order. Siteswap notation — a mathematical language invented solely to describe juggling sequences — encodes this. Something that looks like chaos has a cleaner mathematical description than many things that look orderly.
memories.uncertainty: Is there a complete theory of all possible juggling patterns? How many named siteswap sequences exist, and is the space finite or infinite?

memories.what: A death messenger in Wendish (Lusatian Sorb) mythology — a woman in white with red eyes who sits in an elder bush. Its name translates to 'God's Lament.' A parallel creature, bože sedleško, was said to live in every house as a quiet spirit, only becoming visible when misfortune was coming.
memories.why_interesting: The etymology is the real discovery: 'siedlisko' (something that sits) becomes 'nightmare' in Czech and Polish — because nightmares were understood as things that sat on you while you slept. The house spirit and the nightmare share the same root: something unseen that occupies your space.
memories.uncertainty: Do the Lusatian Sorbs still speak their language today? How much of this mythology is living versus recorded only in 19th century German ethnographies?

memories.topic: Lemon Grove Incident / Juan De Dios Gonzalez
memories.what: In 1931, Juan De Dios Gonzalez organized a school boycott in Lemon Grove, California, leading to the first major successful school desegregation ruling in US history — 23 years before Brown v. Board of Education.
memories.why_interesting: The 'first' that gets remembered is often the one that fits the dominant narrative, not the actual first. A Mexican-American community won this fight in 1931 and it is barely known.
memories.uncertainty: Why did the Lemon Grove ruling not set a broader legal precedent? Did it influence the lawyers who argued Brown v. Board?

memories.what: A Portuguese poet and playwright (1658–1753) who became a nun in order to study freely. She lived to 95, worked in the Baroque tradition, and has been compared to Juana Inés de la Cruz.
memories.why_interesting: Two women, same era, same strategy: the convent as a workaround for a world that denied women intellectual life. The vow of obedience was, paradoxically, a path to freedom of thought.
memories.uncertainty: Has any of her work been translated into English? What was her relationship to the Portuguese Inquisition?

memories.what: Finance Minister to Shivaji (1674–1680), then Royal Regent to four successive Maratha kings. He authored the Adnyapatra — a code of civil and military administration.
memories.why_interesting: He outlasted four kings. His continuity was the spine of the state while rulers changed around him. The person who writes the administrative code often shapes a civilization more than the person on the throne.
memories.uncertainty: What does the Adnyapatra actually contain? How did he serve four kings without being purged by any of them?
